message:
perf(prepare): cache placeholder VdbeProgram in process-wide OnceLock

Replaces the five-branch `build_placeholder_program()` pattern — which
allocated a fresh `Arc<VdbeProgram>` (and memcpy'd an empty `ProgramBuilder`
into it) on every `prepare()` hop that doesn't compile to real bytecode —
with a lazily-initialized process-wide `OnceLock<Arc<VdbeProgram>>`. Each
subsequent call is now a single atomic-refcount-inc `Arc::clone`.

Why this matters:
  The placeholder is what `prepare()` hands to the `PreparedStatement`
  struct when the statement is a SELECT that gets dispatched through the
  planner, or a deferred path that compiles lazily on first `step`. The
  program itself is a pure constant — `ProgramBuilder::new().finish()`
  produces an empty `ops` SmallVec, zero labels, zero bind parameters,
  zero registers — so there is no correctness reason to build it
  per-statement.

  On the 2026-04-23 MT 8-thread DWARF capture
  (`fsqlite-memmove-dwarf-213322`), `build_placeholder_program` was
  attributed 2.66% of self-time inside `__memmove_avx_unaligned_erms`,
  with the memcpy sourced from the `Arc::new(VdbeProgram { .. })` call
  moving the struct into a fresh arc allocation every invocation. The
  cached path removes that memcpy entirely from the hot prepare loop.

Race-safety:
  `OnceLock::get_or_init` means two threads that race to populate will
  both observe the first-winner's arc; the loser's freshly-built program
  is dropped on scope exit. The returned `Arc::clone` is always the
  canonical one.

No behavioral change: same placeholder program, same error propagation
on the (unreachable-in-practice) `ProgramBuilder::finish()` error path,
same signature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
